doa6ReleaseDatePlatforms.title: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Release Date, Platforms, and Editions
doa6ReleaseDatePlatforms.subtitle: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round is the current-generation version of Dead or Alive 6 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam. Use this section to separate the 2026 Last Round release from the original 2019 platform details.
doa6ReleaseDatePlatforms.intro: This module gives players the practical release details first: where the game is playable, which edition to pick, what the standard version includes, and what carries over from Dead or Alive 6. It also highlights the main Last Round changes: 29 standard playable fighters, Photo Mode, current-generation hardware support, and transfer support for save data and many previously purchased costumes.

items.label: Current Status
items.detail: Digital download release for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam.

items.label: Official Release Date
items.detail: KOEI TECMO lists the release as 25/06/2026. The Steam store page displays Jun 24, 2026 for its store timing.

items.label: Supported Platforms
items.detail: Last Round is updated for current-generation hardware and is separate from the original 2019 Dead or Alive 6 release on PS4, Xbox One, and Steam.

items.label: Standard Edition
items.detail: The standard listing includes DOA6 Last Round Core Fighters, the Story Unlock Key, and a 25 Character Unlock Key.

items.label: Core Fighters Edition
items.detail: Core Fighters includes Kasumi, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO as playable fighters. Photo Mode, DOA Quest, DOA Central, and online play are also available.

items.label: Included Former DLC Fighters
items.detail: These five DOA6 DLC fighters are included in the Last Round standard roster, bringing the standard roster total to 29 playable characters.

items.label: Separate Guest Unlocks
items.detail: Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond require separate unlock keys and are not part of the 29-character standard set.

items.label: Carry-Over Support
items.value: Save data and many DOA6 costumes
items.detail: Carry-over uses the same platform account. Last Round data does not transfer back to the original DOA6, and original DOA6 does not crossplay with Last Round.

items.label: New Last Round Features
items.value: Photo Mode, new costumes, hardware update
items.detail: Photo Mode adds character position and pose tools. Kasumi, Ayane, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO receive new Team NINJA themed costumes.

items.label: Players
items.detail: Store listings show local play and online multiplayer support, with online player counts listed up to 16.

doa6CharactersTierList.title: Dead or Alive 6 Character Roster and Tier List
doa6CharactersTierList.subtitle: Browse the Last Round roster by fighter, style, unlock status, and practical tier placement. The grid separates standard roster fighters from separate guest unlocks.
doa6CharactersTierList.intro: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round standard edition includes the original 24 DOA6 fighters plus Nyotengu, Phase 4, Momiji, Rachel, and Tamaki. Core Fighters starts with Kasumi, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO, while Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond are handled as separate guest character unlocks.

tiers.label: Top Priority Picks
tiers.description: Fast pressure, flexible confirms, strong hold tools, or clean matchup coverage.

fighters.name: Kasumi
fighters.unlock: Playable in Core Fighters; included in Standard

fighters.name: NiCO
fighters.role: Fast technical pressure with EMF-enhanced attacks
fighters.unlock: Playable in Core Fighters; included in Standard

tiers.label: Strong Core Picks
tiers.description: Reliable characters with strong damage routes, strong neutral tools, or clear game plans.

fighters.name: Marie Rose
fighters.unlock: Playable in Core Fighters; included in Standard

tiers.label: Solid and Matchup-Ready
tiers.description: Good roster choices that reward matchup knowledge, spacing discipline, or character-specific routing.

fighters.name: Honoka
fighters.role: Mixed move set and stance power
fighters.unlock: Playable in Core Fighters; included in Standard

tiers.label: Specialist Picks
tiers.description: Characters with strong identity who need more deliberate spacing, throw reads, stance use, or matchup experience.

tiers.label: Separate Character Unlocks
tiers.description: Guest fighters shown on official Last Round character pages and store add-on listings, handled separately from the 29-character standard roster.

doa6BeginnerGuideControls.title: Dead or Alive 6 Beginner Guide: Controls, Defense, and First Practice Flow
doa6BeginnerGuideControls.subtitle: Start with the core buttons, learn the strike-throw-hold triangle, then add the Special button and Break Gauge. This guide gives new players a clean route from controls to first-match fundamentals.
doa6BeginnerGuideControls.intro: Dead or Alive 6 uses five main actions: Punch, Kick, Hold, Throw, and Special. Once those are understood, new players should practice the triangle system, high/mid/low defense, Break Hold, Break Blow, Fatal Rush, and simple combo routing in Tutorial, Free Training, Command Training, and Combo Challenge.

steps.title: Learn the default buttons first
steps.description: Memorize Punch, Kick, Hold, Throw, and Special before learning character-specific strings.

steps.title: Understand the triangle system
steps.description: Strikes beat throws, throws beat holds, and holds beat strikes. Winning the correct interaction deals extra damage, so the goal is not just pressing faster; it is reading what the opponent is trying to do.

steps.title: Practice high, mid, and low defense
steps.description: Standing guard blocks high and mid strikes, crouching guard blocks low strikes, and holds must match the attack height. High Hold answers high strikes, Mid Punch Hold answers mid punches, Mid Kick Hold answers mid kicks, and Low Hold answers low strikes.

steps.title: Use the Special button for beginner tools
steps.description: The Special button connects key beginner actions: sidestepping, Side Attack, Fatal Rush, Break Hold, and Break Blow. It is the fastest way to access the new DOA6 systems while learning the command list.

steps.title: Spend Break Gauge carefully
steps.description: Break Gauge fills during a fight. At 50%, use Break Hold to counter any strike height. At 100%, use Break Blow for a powerful strike through the opponent's attack.

steps.title: Build a first combo route
steps.description: Start with one safe string, one launcher, two air hits, and one knockback ender. Keep the route short until you can repeat it from both sides of the screen.

steps.title: Use the training modes in order
steps.description: Run Tutorial for system basics, Free Training for movement and defense, Command Training for each fighter's inputs, Combo Challenge for routes, and DOA Quest for practical missions and rewards.

steps.title: Use a simple first-match checklist
steps.description: Block more than you mash, throw opponents who hold too much, hold opponents who repeat obvious strikes, and save Break Hold for pressure that would otherwise trap you in stun.

doa6CombatSystemMechanics.title: Dead or Alive 6 Combat System: Break Gauge, Fatal Rush, Holds, and Stage Hazards
doa6CombatSystemMechanics.subtitle: Learn each system one at a time: triangle rules first, Break Gauge second, stage control third. This module breaks the combat system into short mechanics cards for quick learning.
doa6CombatSystemMechanics.intro: Dead or Alive 6 is built around reads, counters, and positional damage. Strikes, throws, and holds form the core triangle; Break Gauge adds Break Hold and Break Blow; Fatal Rush gives beginners a simple combo route; and Danger Zones turn stage position into real damage.

mechanics.title: Triangle System
mechanics.summary: The core read system: strikes beat throws, throws beat holds, and holds beat strikes.
details: A correct interaction deals increased damage.
Use strikes when the opponent tries to throw.
Use throws when the opponent relies on holds.
Use holds when the opponent repeats predictable strikes.
mechanics.example: After blocking a repeated string, try a throw if the opponent keeps fishing for holds.

mechanics.title: High, Mid, and Low Attacks
mechanics.summary: Every attack height matters because holds must match the incoming attack type.
details: High attacks work well against standing opponents.
Mid and low attacks are effective against crouching opponents.
High Hold, Mid Punch Hold, Mid Kick Hold, and Low Hold cover different strike types.
mechanics.example: When an opponent repeats a low kick ender, switch from standing guard to crouching guard or Low Hold.

mechanics.title: Break Gauge
mechanics.summary: The special meter used for Break Hold and Break Blow.
details: The gauge fills during the fight as attacks are dealt and received.
50% Break Gauge enables Break Hold.
100% Break Gauge enables Break Blow.
mechanics.example: Save 50% meter to escape heavy pressure, or save 100% meter for a round-changing Break Blow.

mechanics.title: Fatal Rush
mechanics.summary: A simple Special-button combo that can reach up to four strikes.
details: The fourth hit sends the opponent flying and causes Fatal Stun.
With full Break Gauge, the final strike can become a Break Blow.
It gives new players a simple route into damage while they learn deeper commands.
mechanics.example: Use Fatal Rush as a starter route, then replace pieces with stronger character-specific combos as you learn the fighter.

mechanics.title: Break Blow
mechanics.summary: A powerful Break Gauge attack used at 100% meter.
details: Break Blow is designed to hit through the opponent's attack.
It can be cancelled after the first hit lands, leaving the opponent open for a follow-up combo.
It is best used when the opponent is committed or when you can confirm the opening.
mechanics.example: Use Break Blow to punish a predictable attack, then practice the cancel route in Free Training.

mechanics.title: Break Hold
mechanics.summary: A universal hold that uses 50% Break Gauge.
details: Break Hold works against high, mid, and low strikes.
It can be used during Critical Stun and Fatal Stun.
Use it as a defensive escape when pressure or stun would otherwise keep you locked down.
mechanics.example: When trapped in Fatal Stun, use Break Hold to stop the opponent's pressure.

mechanics.title: Side Attack and Sidestep
mechanics.summary: Sidestep avoids straight-line attacks, and Side Attack converts that movement into offense.
details: Side Attack is performed while sidestepping.
It avoids straight-line attacks only.
Use it against linear pressure, not against every move on instinct.
mechanics.example: Against a player who repeats a straight advancing strike, sidestep into Side Attack instead of blocking forever.

mechanics.title: Guards, Throws, and Hold Punishes
mechanics.summary: Blocking is safe against strikes, but throws punish passive blocking and failed holds.
details: Standing guard covers high and mid strikes.
Crouching guard covers low strikes.
Throws beat holds and are ineffective against strikes.
Standard throws can be escaped when timed correctly.
mechanics.example: When the opponent freezes after your pressure, throw. When they mash fast strikes, block or hold.

mechanics.title: Danger Zones and Stage Hazards
mechanics.summary: Many stages include zones that deal extra damage when fighters are knocked into them.
details: Danger Zones include walls, floors, explosions, cars, and other stage-specific hazards.
Rumble Danger can push a fighter back into the ring and create an attack opening.
Mass-destruction objects such as boxes and jars can break for extra damage.
mechanics.example: Use knockback strings near a wall or hazard instead of always ending combos in open space.

mechanics.title: Launch and Basic Combo Routing
mechanics.summary: Simple routes are easier to repeat: stun, launch, juggle, ender, then reset spacing.
details: Use pressure or a counter hit to create stun.
Launch after the opponent is vulnerable.
Add a short air combo, then finish with knockback or wall carry.
Keep early routes consistent before chasing max-damage combos.
mechanics.example: Practice one route midscreen and one route near a wall before adding character-specific variants.

doa6StoryModeDoaQuest.title: Dead or Alive 6 Story Mode and DOA Quest
doa6StoryModeDoaQuest.subtitle: Track Story Mode, DOA Quest missions, unlock keys, rewards, and the best order to play each mode.
doa6StoryModeDoaQuest.intro: Story Mode delivers the main narrative, cinematics, and character substories, while DOA Quest turns the fighting system into mission-based challenges with practical rewards. This section separates full-game access from Core Fighters access so players know exactly what is playable before buying unlock keys.

modes.bestFor: Following the main DOA6 story, cinematics, and character-focused substories.
modes.access: Included in the paid PS4, Xbox One, and Steam versions of DEAD OR ALIVE 6.
modes.coreFightersAccess: DEAD OR ALIVE 6: Core Fighters includes introductory story access and requires the Story Unlock Key for full Story Mode.
modes.lastRoundCoreFightersAccess: DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round Core Fighters excludes Story Mode until the Story Unlock Key is purchased.
modes.characterRule: Story Mode can use story-required characters even when their Core Fighters character licenses are not owned.

modes.bestFor: Learning game systems through short mission objectives.
modes.access: Available in the paid game, DEAD OR ALIVE 6: Core Fighters, and DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round Core Fighters.
modes.missionStructure: Quests are made up of several missions that ask players to clear specific objectives.
modes.characterRule: All characters can be used in DOA Quest even without their Core Fighters character licenses.

modes.bestFor: Preparing for uncleared DOA Quest missions and character-specific tasks.
modes.access: Training modes include Free Training, Tutorial, Command Training, and Combo Challenge.
modes.missionUse: Tutorial can be checked to find uncleared mission topics before retrying DOA Quest objectives.

modes.bestFor: Spending earned Owner Points and managing unlocked content.
modes.access: Available in the full game and Core Fighters versions.
modes.rewardUse: Owner Points can be used to unlock and purchase costumes, hairstyles, accessories, titles, BGM, encyclopedia entries, and trivia entries.

modes.bestFor: New players who want progression and rewards without wasting unlock purchases.
modes.access: Start with Tutorial, move into DOA Quest, then play Story Mode after confirming Story access.
route: DOA Quest for missions and rewards
Story Mode for cinematics and substories
DOA Central for spending Owner Points

doa6CostumesDlcSeasonPasses.title: Dead or Alive 6 Costumes DLC and Season Passes
doa6CostumesDlcSeasonPasses.subtitle: Compare Season Passes, costume sets, character DLC, Premium Tickets, and Last Round carry-over rules.
doa6CostumesDlcSeasonPasses.intro: DEAD OR ALIVE 6 DLC is split across Season Passes, character unlocks, costume sets, Premium Tickets, and Last Round carry-over rules. This module groups the official packs by purpose so players can avoid duplicate purchases, locked costumes, and account-transfer mistakes.

packs.name: Season Pass 1
costumeSets: Pirates of the 7 Seas Costumes Vol. 1
Pirates of the 7 Seas Costumes Vol. 2
packs.note: Season Pass 1 includes its listed character and costume content, so players should not buy the same included packs separately.

packs.name: Season Pass 2
packs.note: Vacation to Paradise BGM Set and the listed costume packs are part of Season Pass 2.

packs.name: Season Pass 3
packs.note: Rachel and her debut costume set are included in Season Pass 3.

packs.name: Season Pass 4
packs.note: Tamaki and her debut costumes are included in Season Pass 4.

packs.name: Original DOA6 Character DLC
packs.note: Core Fighters players need character licenses to use locked characters outside modes that allow all characters.

packs.name: Premium Tickets
packs.use: Premium Tickets were introduced for unlocking individual DLC costumes in the Wardrobe on PS4.
limitations: Premium Tickets are tied to the purchasing account.
They are not shared across different PS4 accounts.
Premium Tickets from DEAD OR ALIVE 6 on PS4 cannot be used in DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round.
packs.note: Check remaining Premium Tickets before buying direct costume DLC, because direct costume purchases can leave unused tickets behind.

packs.name: DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round Carry-over
packs.note: A character Unlock Key is required to use carried-over costumes for a locked character.

packs.name: Last Round KOF Collaboration DLC
contents: Mai Shiranui Character Unlock Key & Debut Costume Bundle
Kula Diamond Character Unlock Key & Debut Costume Bundle
packs.note: Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond are not included in the Last Round full version and must be purchased separately.

doa6CoreFightersFreeVersion.title: Dead or Alive 6 Core Fighters and Free Version
doa6CoreFightersFreeVersion.subtitle: Compare the original Core Fighters version, Last Round Core Fighters, and paid editions before choosing an upgrade path.
doa6CoreFightersFreeVersion.intro: Core Fighters is the free-to-play entry point for DEAD OR ALIVE 6, but the free roster and Story Mode rules changed in DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round. This table shows what players can use immediately, what needs an unlock key, and what the paid versions include.

rows.paid: Base roster included, with Nyotengu and Phase 4 requiring separate access depending on edition and purchase path.
rows.lrPaid: 29 characters: the original 24 characters plus Nyotengu, Phase 4, Momiji, Rachel, and Tamaki.

rows.paid: Nyotengu, Phase 4, and later DLC characters require the relevant character purchase when not included in the owned edition.
rows.core: Most characters require separate character licenses or character sets.
rows.lrPaid: Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond require separate Character Unlock Keys.
rows.lrCore: Every character outside Kasumi, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO requires a Character Unlock Key.

rows.core: Introductory story access is playable; the Story Unlock Key is required for full Story Mode.
rows.lrPaid: Included through the full version's Story Unlock Key.
rows.lrCore: Story Mode requires the Story Unlock Key.

rows.paid: Ranked Match and Lobby Match are available.
rows.core: Ranked Match and Lobby Match are available with the free roster and owned characters.
rows.lrPaid: Ranked Match and Lobby Match are available.
rows.lrCore: Ranked Match and Lobby Match are available with the free roster and owned characters.

rows.paid: Free Training, Tutorial, Command Training, and Combo Challenge are available.
rows.core: Training modes are available, and Tutorial can use all characters even without their licenses.
rows.lrPaid: Free Training, Tutorial, Command Training, and Combo Training are available.
rows.lrCore: Training modes are available; Tutorial can use all characters even without Unlock Keys.

rows.paid: Wardrobe, Database, Theater, Music, and Library are available.
rows.lrPaid: Wardrobe, Database, Theater, Music, Library, and Photo Mode are available.
rows.lrCore: DOA Central and Photo Mode are available.

rows.paid: Buy character DLC, costume DLC, Season Passes, or edition bundles as needed.
rows.core: Buy the Story Unlock Key, character licenses, character sets, costume DLC, or Deluxe content.
rows.lrPaid: Full version includes Story Unlock Key and Character Unlock Keys for the 29 included characters.
rows.lrCore: Buy the Story Unlock Key, individual Character Unlock Keys, KOF character unlocks, or the full Last Round version.

doa6PcRequirementsOnlineCrossplay.title: Dead or Alive 6 PC Requirements, Online Modes, and Crossplay
doa6PcRequirementsOnlineCrossplay.subtitle: Check Steam requirements, online mode limits, save transfer, DLC carry-over, and crossplay restrictions before buying or moving to Last Round.
doa6PcRequirementsOnlineCrossplay.intro: DEAD OR ALIVE 6 and DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round have different PC requirements, store status, and transfer rules. This table keeps the important platform details in one place for Steam, PlayStation, and Xbox players.

rows.details: The original Steam page remains visible, but the game is no longer available for purchase on the Steam store.
Available digitally on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam.

specs.label: CPU
specs.value: Intel Core i5-8400 or higher / AMD Ryzen 3 3100 or higher

specs.label: GPU
specs.value: GeForce GTX 1060 VRAM 6GB or higher / Radeon RX 590 VRAM 8GB or higher

specs.label: Target
specs.value: 1280x720 at 60 FPS with low or disabled graphics options

specs.label: CPU
specs.value: Intel Core i7-8700 or higher / AMD Ryzen 5 3600XT or higher

specs.label: GPU
specs.value: GeForce GTX 1070 VRAM 8GB or higher / Radeon RX 5700 XT VRAM 8GB or higher

specs.label: Target
specs.value: 1920x1080 at 60 FPS with high graphics options

rows.details: Fight players of the same rank online. PS4 connection quality can be changed by the player, Xbox One uses Xbox Live Smart Match, and Steam connection quality cannot be changed.
Create lobbies with custom rules and private rooms. No Grade Points are gained or lost in Lobby Match.
PlayStation Store lists PS Plus required for PS5 online play and supports up to 16 online players. Xbox lists online multiplayer for 2 to 16 players.
Crossplay between different platforms is not supported in DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round.

rows.item: DOA6 and Last Round network split
rows.details: There is no crossplay between DEAD OR ALIVE 6 and DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round.

DEAD OR ALIVE 6 save data, progression, and items can transfer to Last Round from the same account. Transfer requires the same platform account, cannot go back to the original DOA6, and existing Last Round save data must be deleted first.
PS5 detects local save data and receives PS4 saves via USB or PS Plus cloud. Xbox Series X|S transfers via Xbox Network. Steam transfers from the same PC, Steam Cloud, or the local save folder.
Most previously purchased DOA6 costumes transfer to Last Round, but the matching character Unlock Key is required to use a carried-over costume for a locked character.
DLC purchased for DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round cannot be transferred back to DEAD OR ALIVE 6.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Dead or Alive 6 Last Round

questions.question: Is Dead or Alive 6 Last Round a free upgrade from Dead or Alive 6?
questions.answer: No. Dead or Alive 6 Last Round is the separate current-generation version for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam, with its own store listing, 29-character standard roster, and Photo Mode.

questions.question: How many playable characters are in Dead or Alive 6 Last Round?
questions.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round includes 29 standard playable characters: the original 24 DOA6 fighters plus Nyotengu, Phase 4, Momiji, Rachel, and Tamaki. Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond are separate guest unlocks.

questions.question: What platforms is Dead or Alive 6 Last Round available on?
questions.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round is available on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam. The original Dead or Alive 6 launched on PS4, Xbox One, and Steam.

questions.question: Does Dead or Alive 6 support crossplay between platforms?
questions.answer: No. Dead or Alive 6 Last Round does not support crossplay between different platforms, and there is no crossplay between Dead or Alive 6 and Dead or Alive 6 Last Round.

questions.question: What is Dead or Alive 6 Core Fighters?
questions.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Core Fighters is the free-to-play version. Last Round Core Fighters includes Kasumi, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO, plus DOA Quest, DOA Central, Photo Mode, and online Ranked and Lobby matches.

questions.question: Can I transfer my Dead or Alive 6 save data to Last Round?
questions.answer: Yes. Dead or Alive 6 save data, progression, and most costumes transfer to Last Round from the same platform account. Transfer is one-way only and most DLC costumes carry over with the matching character Unlock Key.

dlc.whatIs: What is Dead or Alive 6 Last Round DLC?
dlc.whatIsDescription: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round DLC spans Season Passes, costume sets, the Digital Deluxe Edition content, Core Fighters, free characters, and the free version, with clear carry-over rules so players avoid duplicate purchases.

whySteps.step3Description: Try Core Fighters and free characters before you buy

unlockables.whatIs: What are Dead or Alive 6 Last Round unlockables?
